libro: AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01) Study Guide

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 2: AWS Fundamentals for the AI Practitioner

preguntas.pregunta: What is the definition of the shared responsibility model?

respuestas.a: AWS is responsible for all security aspects of cloud applications.
respuestas.b: Customers are responsible for security in the cloud, while AWS manages security of the cloud.
respuestas.c: AWS only provides security updates if customers request them.
respuestas.d: Customers are required to manage physical security at AWS data centers.

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following is an AWS infrastructure as a service (IaaS)?

respuestas.a: AWS Lambda
respuestas.b: Amazon EC2
respuestas.c: Amazon RDS
respuestas.d: Amazon Chime

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service provides virtual machines (VMs)?

respuestas.a: Amazon RDS
respuestas.b: Amazon S3
respuestas.c: Amazon EC2
respuestas.d: AWS Glue

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of the public cloud?

respuestas.a: The customer has full control over infrastructure and customization.
respuestas.b: There are lower costs due to shared resources.
respuestas.c: You have guaranteed security against all cybersecurity threats.
respuestas.d: There is a single-tenant model for each organization.

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following best defines cloud computing?

respuestas.a: A set of physical servers that companies purchase and manage internally
respuestas.b: The ability to access IT resources on demand over the internet
respuestas.c: A private data center managed by a single company
respuestas.d: A high-performance computing system for AI applications

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM)?

respuestas.a: To encrypt all AWS storage resources
respuestas.b: To manage user authentication and authorization for AWS services
respuestas.c: To provide monitoring and logging for AWS environments
respuestas.d: To automatically back up AWS resources for disaster recovery purposes

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following best describes the role of feature engineering in machine learning (ML)?

respuestas.a: It creates new variables or transforms data to improve model performance.
respuestas.b: It trains the model using labeled data.
respuestas.c: It evaluates the accuracy of a trained model.
respuestas.d: It ensures that the model is not biased.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the primary purpose of reinforcement learning in AI?

respuestas.a: To learn patterns from labeled data
respuestas.b: To optimize decisions based on rewards and penalties
respuestas.c: To detect anomalies in datasets
respuestas.d: To reduce dimensionality in high-dimensional datasets

preguntas.pregunta: A company is using Amazon SageMaker to build a machine learning (ML) model. What is the primary advantage of using SageMaker over traditional on- premises ML infrastructure?

respuestas.a: It eliminates the need for data preprocessing.
respuestas.b: It requires more manual intervention than on-premises solutions.
respuestas.c: It provides pretrained models that cannot be customized.
respuestas.d: It automates the entire ML lifecycle, from training to deployment.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the primary difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?

respuestas.a: Supervised learning does not require labeled data, while unsupervised learning does.
respuestas.b: Supervised learning focuses on reinforcement learning, while unsupervised learning does not.
respuestas.c: Supervised learning uses labeled data, while unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data.
respuestas.d: Supervised learning is only used for classification tasks, while unsupervised learning is used for all other ML applications.

preguntas.pregunta: A retailer wants to group its customers based on purchasing behavior without using predefined labels. Which machine learning (ML) approach should they use?

respuestas.a: Reinforcement learning
respuestas.b: Supervised learning
respuestas.c: Unsupervised learning
respuestas.d: Semisupervised learning

preguntas.pregunta: Why is model monitoring important in machine learning (ML)?

respuestas.a: It prevents overfitting by reducing the number of features in a dataset.
respuestas.b: It ensures that a deployed model maintains accuracy and adapts to data changes.
respuestas.c: It eliminates the need for retraining models over time.
respuestas.d: It guarantees that predictions will always be correct.

preguntas.pregunta: Which generative AI model involves two neural networks that compete against each other?

respuestas.a: Variational autoencoder (VAE)
respuestas.b: Transformer model
respuestas.c: Generative adversarial network (GAN)
respuestas.d: Diffusion model

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of positional encoding for a transformer model?

respuestas.a: To enhance the performance of backpropagation
respuestas.b: To process words in the original order of the text
respuestas.c: To improve the reliability of GPUs
respuestas.d: To make AI models more cost-effective

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) compared to the process of fine-tuning?

respuestas.a: RAG requires less energy.
respuestas.b: It significantly increases the model’s speed.
respuestas.c: RAG mitigates bias in AI responses.
respuestas.d: It does not require the modification of a model’s internal weights.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of a transformer model over a recurrent neural network (RNN)?

respuestas.a: Transformers require no labeled data.
respuestas.b: Transformers can process entire datasets in parallel.
respuestas.c: RNNs cannot process text.
respuestas.d: Transformers do not require training.

preguntas.pregunta: Why does an AI model create hallucinations?

respuestas.a: They rely on probabilistic predictions.
respuestas.b: They use GPUs, which can be unpredictable.
respuestas.c: They are based on supervised learning.
respuestas.d: They are only a problem with small models.

preguntas.pregunta: Which part of a neural network allows for detecting patterns in a dataset?

respuestas.a: Input layer
respuestas.b: Output layer
respuestas.c: Hidden layer
respuestas.d: Activation layer

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 5: Real-World AI Applications with AWS Tools

preguntas.pregunta: What is the difference between intelligent document processing (IDP) and natural language processing (NLP)?

respuestas.a: NLP is only used for chatbots, while IDP is used for all text-based AI applications.
respuestas.b: NLP is a subset of IDP.
respuestas.c: IDP automates data extraction for business documents, while NLP is for broader text-processing tasks.
respuestas.d: NLP is only for handwritten language, while IDP is for printed language.

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service would you use for translating text into different languages?

respuestas.a: Amazon Comprehend
respuestas.b: Amazon Polly
respuestas.c: Amazon Translate
respuestas.d: Amazon Textract

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of Amazon Kendra?

respuestas.a: It uses AI models to translate foreign languages.
respuestas.b: It converts text into speech.
respuestas.c: It uses AI to create financial reports.
respuestas.d: It provides an AI-powered search-and-retrieval system that processes natural language queries.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a core AI capability in Amazon Kendra?

respuestas.a: It retrieves data from a quantum database.
respuestas.b: It uses linear regression analysis.
respuestas.c: It uses retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to improve search accuracy.
respuestas.d: It uses keyword matching.

preguntas.pregunta: What does Amazon Transcribe do?

respuestas.a: It converts spoken audio into written text.
respuestas.b: It converts text into another language.
respuestas.c: It extracts information from a document.
respuestas.d: It provides for sentiment analysis of text.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of Amazon Polly?

respuestas.a: It transcribes spoken audio into text.
respuestas.b: It translates text into other languages.
respuestas.c: It extracts insights from large databases for analytics.
respuestas.d: It converts text into humanlike speech.

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 6: Building with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Q

preguntas.pregunta: For an AI model in Amazon Bedrock, what might happen if you adjust the temperature and Top P?

respuestas.a: Using both will guarantee more accurate responses.
respuestas.b: Using a combination of these may cause unpredictable or unexpected responses.
respuestas.c: Using both will disable the model.
respuestas.d: Using both will make the responses more deterministic.

preguntas.pregunta: When configuring the image/video playground in Amazon Bedrock, what is a “negative prompt”?

respuestas.a: This increases the model’s temperature.
respuestas.b: It generates black-and-white images.
respuestas.c: It specifies elements you want to exclude from the image or video.
respuestas.d: This decreases the size of the image.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the role of the modality filter in Amazon Bedrock’s model catalog?

respuestas.a: The filter lets users sort models by license type.
respuestas.b: It filters models based on language support.
respuestas.c: The filter identifies models that support serverless deployment.
respuestas.d: It categorizes models by input and output types like text, image, audio, and multimodal.

preguntas.pregunta: For a model in Amazon Bedrock, why would you increase the setting for temperature?

respuestas.a: To generate deterministic content
respuestas.b: To produce videos
respuestas.c: To generate creative content
respuestas.d: To summarize information

preguntas.pregunta: For a model profile in Amazon Bedrock, what type of information would you usually find?

respuestas.a: Type of GPU used
respuestas.b: Version, release date, deployment type, modalities, and model ID
respuestas.c: Only the license information
respuestas.d: The datasets

preguntas.pregunta: What do you need to do to gain access to foundation models (FMs) in Amazon Bedrock that are not enabled by default?

respuestas.a: Enable “On-demand” and restart the session.
respuestas.b: Submit a model access request form with use case details.
respuestas.c: Complete an AWS certification exam.
respuestas.d: Download the model on your own computer.

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 7: A Guide to Prompt Engineering

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following is a required component of every prompt when working with a foundation model (FM)?

respuestas.a: Context
respuestas.b: Input data
respuestas.c: Instruction
respuestas.d: Output indicator

preguntas.pregunta: What is the reason for the output indicator in a prompt?

respuestas.a: To provide relevant examples of how to write better prompts
respuestas.b: To specify the format or structure of the model’s response
respuestas.c: To provide background information for the task
respuestas.d: To train the model on a new dataset

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of context in a prompt?

respuestas.a: To supply relevant input data
respuestas.b: To provide background that improves response quality
respuestas.c: To specify the output format
respuestas.d: To summarize past interactions with the model

preguntas.pregunta: Why would you use delimiters like ### in a prompt?

respuestas.a: They minimize the use of tokens.
respuestas.b: They separate the formatting options.
respuestas.c: They clearly distinguish between instructions and input data.
respuestas.d: They improve model performance for follow-up prompts.

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following best demonstrates a persona-based context?

respuestas.a: “Write the blog post in under 500 words.”
respuestas.b: “You are a procurement analyst at a mid-sized manufacturing company.”
respuestas.c: “These are the product reviews to analyze.”
respuestas.d: “Format the results as a bar chart.”

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following is an example of few-shot prompting?

respuestas.a: “Translate this blog into German.”
respuestas.b: “List three benefits of generative AI.”
respuestas.c: “Here are examples of customer support feedback and their categories. Now categorize this new inquiry.”
respuestas.d: “Let’s break this down step by step.”

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 8: A Framework for Responsible AI

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following methods can help reduce overfitting in an AI model?

respuestas.a: Increasing the complexity of the model
respuestas.b: Adding more noisy data
respuestas.c: Stopping training early
respuestas.d: Avoiding hyperparameters

preguntas.pregunta: What is one technique companies use to address intellectual property concerns in generative AI?

respuestas.a: Removing all training data from public sources
respuestas.b: Restricting access to AI tools
respuestas.c: Limiting AI to internal company use only
respuestas.d: Creating licensing agreements with content providers

preguntas.pregunta: Why is accuracy in AI models considered key to responsible AI?

respuestas.a: It improves reliability, trust, and safety.
respuestas.b: Accurate models require fewer updates and patches.
respuestas.c: Accuracy makes models less costly.
respuestas.d: Accuracy only matters for visual models.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the main purpose of fairness in AI systems?

respuestas.a: Increasing the sophistication of models
respuestas.b: Reducing latency in decision making
respuestas.c: Enhancing personalization features
respuestas.d: Avoiding discrimination against individuals or groups

preguntas.pregunta: How does explainability compare to transparency?

respuestas.a: Explainability focuses on user interface design.
respuestas.b: Explainability explains model decisions; transparency shares system details.
respuestas.c: They mean the same thing.
respuestas.d: Transparency is only required in open source models.

preguntas.pregunta: What do privacy and security in AI primarily aim to protect?

respuestas.a: Model weights and parameters
respuestas.b: Algorithm transparency
respuestas.c: Individual data and usage control
respuestas.d: Developer intellectual property

capitulos.seccion: Capítulo 9: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key difference between governance and compliance?

respuestas.a: Governance enforces laws, while compliance manages innovation.
respuestas.b: Governance protects data, while compliance ensures ethical AI development.
respuestas.c: Governance guides decision making and risk management, while compliance ensures adherence to external and internal rules.
respuestas.d: Governance is optional, but compliance is legally mandatory for all companies.

preguntas.pregunta: In AWS, what best describes the purpose of a defense-in-depth strategy?

respuestas.a: To rely on a single strong security control.
respuestas.b: To use multiple, layered security measures to catch threats that bypass one control.
respuestas.c: To automate model training and inference pipelines.
respuestas.d: To combine multiple security layers so if one fails, others can provide protection.

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS services would you primarily use to protect applications against denial-of-service (DoS) attacks and manage secure user sign-ins?

respuestas.a: Amazon GuardDuty and AWS Private CA
respuestas.b: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) and AWS Certificate Manager (ACM)
respuestas.c: AWS Shield and Amazon Cognito
respuestas.d: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) and AWS WAF

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service helps route sensitive traffic privately without exposing it to the public internet?

respuestas.a: AWS PrivateLink
respuestas.b: AWS Security Hub
respuestas.c: Amazon VPC
respuestas.d: Amazon GuardDuty

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following is an example of a governance policy for AI solutions?

respuestas.a: Building AI models without any human review
respuestas.b: Using only public datasets without checking privacy concerns
respuestas.c: Defining standards for data sourcing, model training, evaluation, and deployment approvals
respuestas.d: Allowing unrestricted model deployment to speed innovation

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS-supported compliance framework is specifically focused on protecting United States healthcare information?

respuestas.a: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
respuestas.b: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
respuestas.c: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
respuestas.d: European Union Agency for Cybersecurity (ENISA)

preguntas.pregunta: What is an availability zone (AZ)?

respuestas.a: A physical data center containing multiple AWS servers
respuestas.b: A logical partition of AWS infrastructure that provides redundancy
respuestas.c: A security group that isolates AWS resources
respuestas.d: A region that spans multiple continents

preguntas.pregunta: Which cloud service model provides full applications to users without requiring them to manage infrastructure?

respuestas.a: Infrastructure as a service (IaaS)
respuestas.b: Platform as a service (PaaS)
respuestas.c: Software as a service (SaaS)
respuestas.d: Virtualization as a service (VaaS)

preguntas.pregunta: What is a defining characteristic of the hybrid cloud model?

respuestas.a: It exclusively uses private data centers for all workloads.
respuestas.b: It combines public and private cloud environments.
respuestas.c: It relies only on on-premises infrastructure.
respuestas.d: It uses a multitenant model for security.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of AWS regions?

respuestas.a: They eliminate the need for availability zones (AZs).
respuestas.b: They allow customers to comply with data residency requirements.
respuestas.c: They are only available in Asia.
respuestas.d: They provide unlimited computing power without redundancy.

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service allows developers to deploy applications without managing servers?

respuestas.a: Amazon EC2
respuestas.b: AWS Lambda
respuestas.c: Amazon RDS
respuestas.d: Amazon CloudFront

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following best describes Amazon S3?

respuestas.a: A relational database service for structured data storage
respuestas.b: A scalable object storage service designed for durability and availability
respuestas.c: A high-performance compute service for running applications
respuestas.d: A content delivery network (CDN) for accelerating web traffic

preguntas.pregunta: What is the main benefit of using a confusion matrix to evaluate a machine learning (ML) model?

respuestas.a: It shows the training time of the model.
respuestas.b: It helps analyze false positives and false negatives.
respuestas.c: It calculates the total number of data points used in training.
respuestas.d: It eliminates the need for additional performance metrics.

preguntas.pregunta: A bank wants to detect fraudulent transactions in real time. What type of inference should they use?

respuestas.a: Batch inference
respuestas.b: Asynchronous inference
respuestas.c: Real-time inference
respuestas.d: On-demand inference

preguntas.pregunta: What is a major challenge when using high-dimensional datasets in machine learning (ML)?

respuestas.a: It reduces the need for model tuning.
respuestas.b: It increases computational costs and complexity.
respuestas.c: It makes models interpret data more efficiently.
respuestas.d: It ensures better accuracy for all ML tasks.

preguntas.pregunta: A company notices that its deployed machine learning (ML) model is becoming less accurate over time due to changing customer behavior. What issue is this?

respuestas.a: Model overfitting
respuestas.b: Hyperparameter tuning issue
respuestas.c: Feature engineering error
respuestas.d: Drift

preguntas.pregunta: What is the primary reason for using Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor?

respuestas.a: To train deep-learning models faster
respuestas.b: To automatically deploy machine learning (ML) models
respuestas.c: To detect issues such as data drift and concept drift
respuestas.d: To fine-tune pretrained models

preguntas.pregunta: A business wants to improve the efficiency of their data processing pipeline by automating feature extraction and transformation. Which AWS tool is best suited for this?

respuestas.a: Amazon Rekognition
respuestas.b: Amazon Textract
respuestas.c: Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler
respuestas.d: AWS Glue

preguntas.pregunta: A software company wants to analyze customer feedback to determine whether reviews are positive, neutral, or negative. Which AWS service should they use?

respuestas.a: Amazon Textract
respuestas.b: Amazon Comprehend
respuestas.c: AWS Lambda
respuestas.d: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store

preguntas.pregunta: What is the primary purpose of hyperparameter tuning in machine learning (ML)?

respuestas.a: To create new training data
respuestas.b: To adjust model parameters for improved performance
respuestas.c: To convert categorical data into numerical form
respuestas.d: To speed up the training process

preguntas.pregunta: How do diffusion models work?

respuestas.a: They add and remove noise in various steps.
respuestas.b: They use competing neural networks.
respuestas.c: They only create text.
respuestas.d: They only create sound.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the primary purpose of fine-tuning a foundation model (FM)?

respuestas.a: To make a model more general-purpose
respuestas.b: To customize the model for a specific domain
respuestas.c: To lower the latency
respuestas.d: To train the model from scratch

preguntas.pregunta: How does an encoder work in a transformer model?

respuestas.a: It creates synthetic data.
respuestas.b: It fine-tunes hyperparameters.
respuestas.c: It evaluates model predictions.
respuestas.d: It processes input sequences and extracts meaningful representations.

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following describes a limit on how much a large language model (LLM) can process at a time?

respuestas.a: Overfitting
respuestas.b: Bias
respuestas.c: Context windows
respuestas.d: The discriminator

preguntas.pregunta: What’s the reason for using reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) in generative AI models?

respuestas.a: To improve the alignment the model’s responses with human preferences
respuestas.b: To lower training costs
respuestas.c: To replace deep learning models
respuestas.d: To reduce the latency of the model

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key benefit of a multimodal foundation model (FM)?

respuestas.a: It requires much less data for the training
respuestas.b: It can process and create different types of content like text, images, and videos
respuestas.c: It is more explainable than text-based models
respuestas.d: It does not require GPUs

preguntas.pregunta: Of the choices below, which is a main reason why large language models consume significant compute resources?

respuestas.a: They use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
respuestas.b: They use complex linear algebra.
respuestas.c: They process billions of parameters to generate accurate responses.
respuestas.d: They rely on human feedback and evaluation for all responses.

preguntas.pregunta: Why can RAG help lower hallucinations in AI models?

respuestas.a: It has guardrails for certain types of prompts
respuestas.b: It searches a proprietary database to enhance the response
respuestas.c: It disables the probability system
respuestas.d: It relies only on human supervision

preguntas.pregunta: Among these choices, what is a core capability of Amazon Lex?

respuestas.a: It analyzes data for fraud detection.
respuestas.b: It allows for video analysis.
respuestas.c: It supports intents and slot filling to create conversational AI experiences.
respuestas.d: It automates the extraction of text from scanned documents.

preguntas.pregunta: What can you do with Amazon Comprehend?

respuestas.a: Analyze text to extract insights such as for sentiment, key phrases, and entities.
respuestas.b: Transcribe audio recordings into text.
respuestas.c: Convert text into humanlike speech.
respuestas.d: Translate text into multiple languages.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a major capability of Amazon Rekognition?

respuestas.a: The translation of spoken language into text.
respuestas.b: The use of optical character recognition (OCR) for scanned documents.
respuestas.c: The generation of AI-powered chatbot responses.
respuestas.d: The ability to recognize faces, objects, and scenes in images and videos.

preguntas.pregunta: Among these options, which is a technique used in natural language processing (NLP) preprocessing?

respuestas.a: Automatically correcting all grammatical errors in a sentence.
respuestas.b: Translating text into another language before processing.
respuestas.c: Lemmatization, which reduces words to their root form.
respuestas.d: Applying deepfake detection to verify text authenticity.

preguntas.pregunta: Why is stopword removal used in natural language processing (NLP) preprocessing?

respuestas.a: Stopwords add important meaning to a text.
respuestas.b: It helps remove uncommon words to focus only on frequently used terms.
respuestas.c: Stopwords are removed to reduce text length to speed up processing.
respuestas.d: Removing stopwords like the and is helps improve efficiency without losing essential meaning.

preguntas.pregunta: When is AI not always the best solution for a business use case?

respuestas.a: AI models do not work well in cloud-based environments.
respuestas.b: AI requires human oversight at all times.
respuestas.c: AI can only be used for simple automation tasks.
respuestas.d: AI solutions can be complex and costly when a simpler approach may suffice.

preguntas.pregunta: What is the Compare Models feature in the Bedrock playground?

respuestas.a: It allows for using image and text responses.
respuestas.b: It provides for making longer responses for two different models.
respuestas.c: It combines responses from two models.
respuestas.d: It allows for evaluating the side-by-side responses from two different models.

preguntas.pregunta: Why would you fine-tune a model in Bedrock?

respuestas.a: To reduce compute costs
respuestas.b: To improve latency
respuestas.c: To improve response accuracy for domain-specific scenarios
respuestas.d: To use images

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of using open source foundation models (FMs) in AWS Bedrock?

respuestas.a: They are always free to use.
respuestas.b: They support real-time inference by default.
respuestas.c: They offer transparency, customization, and community innovation.
respuestas.d: They provide the codebase and datasets.

preguntas.pregunta: What’s a benefit of using a distilled model in Bedrock?

respuestas.a: It reduces compute requirements, making it suitable for edge devices.
respuestas.b: It increases the context window.
respuestas.c: It increases the creativity of the responses.
respuestas.d: It guarantees higher accuracy than larger models.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key benefit of multiagent collaboration in Bedrock?

respuestas.a: It relies on distilled models.
respuestas.b: It provides for 100% accuracy.
respuestas.c: It allows different agents to specialize in specific tasks, leading to better problem solving.
respuestas.d: It enables users to edit model weights and biases.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key benefit of batch processing when using Bedrock?

respuestas.a: There is low latency.
respuestas.b: The context window is unlimited.
respuestas.c: There is higher accuracy.
respuestas.d: Lower costs.

preguntas.pregunta: Which technique improves the consistency and clarity of prompts when used repeatedly?

respuestas.a: Few-shot prompting
respuestas.b: Zero-shot prompting
respuestas.c: Chain-of-thought prompting
respuestas.d: Prompt templates

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting?

respuestas.a: To specify for the large language model (LLM) which examples to use
respuestas.b: To limit the response of the large language model (LLM)
respuestas.c: To allow for step-by-step reasoning for complex problems
respuestas.d: To adjust the large language model (LLM) parameters

preguntas.pregunta: Which type of prompting involves providing several examples to help the model learn a pattern?

respuestas.a: Zero-shot prompting
respuestas.b: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting
respuestas.c: Few-shot prompting
respuestas.d: Template prompting

preguntas.pregunta: What is model poisoning?

respuestas.a: This allows a model to process personal health data.
respuestas.b: This is when biased or malicious data is injected into the training process.
respuestas.c: This when a model lacks proper licensing.
respuestas.d: This is where there are conflicting instructions in a single prompt.

preguntas.pregunta: What is exposure as it relates to security risks with foundation models (FMs)?

respuestas.a: The use of the model for consumer apps
respuestas.b: The loss of GPU performance during training
respuestas.c: The unintentional inclusion of sensitive data in training sets
respuestas.d: The failure to generate output within token limits

preguntas.pregunta: What is jailbreaking of a foundation model (FM)?

respuestas.a: Running a model without GPU acceleration
respuestas.b: Resetting a model’s API token for expanding the access
respuestas.c: Deleting a model’s training history
respuestas.d: Tricking the model into bypassing safety and ethical restrictions

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following best describes AI governance?

respuestas.a: A set of marketing strategies for AI adoption
respuestas.b: Policies and oversight structures to guide ethical AI development
respuestas.c: Legal ownership of AI-generated content
respuestas.d: Techniques for building large language models (LLMs)

preguntas.pregunta: What does controllability in AI focus on?

respuestas.a: Reducing computational costs
respuestas.b: Building faster AI systems
respuestas.c: Aligning AI actions with human intent and oversight
respuestas.d: Removing human involvement

preguntas.pregunta: Which of the following is a business advantage of adopting responsible AI practices?

respuestas.a: Enhanced trust and improved brand reputation
respuestas.b: Reduced need for data collection
respuestas.c: Increased dependence on human intervention
respuestas.d: Elimination of all algorithmic errors

preguntas.pregunta: What is the role of Amazon SageMaker Clarify in responsible AI?

respuestas.a: Detecting harmful URLs in user prompts
respuestas.b: Managing compute costs during training
respuestas.c: Identifying and explaining bias in data and models
respuestas.d: Encrypting training data

preguntas.pregunta: What is Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) primarily used for?

respuestas.a: Accelerating training of neural networks
respuestas.b: Creating synthetic data from scratch
respuestas.c: Managing GPUs in SageMaker
respuestas.d: Integrating human reviews into AI workflows

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key advantage of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)?

respuestas.a: It removes the need for model retraining.
respuestas.b: It ensures AI systems remain static.
respuestas.c: It helps models align outputs with human preferences and judgments.
respuestas.d: It eliminates the need for data labeling.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a compliance risk caused by AI systems developing emergent capabilities?

respuestas.a: They strictly follow preprogrammed features with no surprises.
respuestas.b: They may behave unpredictably in ways not planned by the original designers.
respuestas.c: They automatically file compliance reports as they change.
respuestas.d: They prevent the need for any human oversight.

preguntas.pregunta: What is a key characteristic of a regulated workload?

respuestas.a: It only applies to gaming or entertainment systems.
respuestas.b: It only focuses on making systems faster, not safer.
respuestas.c: It only requires encryption of stored data.
respuestas.d: It must follow specific compliance rules due to legal, industry, or safety concerns.

preguntas.pregunta: Why is data logging important in AI systems?

respuestas.a: It helps speed up model training by skipping error checks.
respuestas.b: It captures inputs, outputs, and system events, supporting debugging, monitoring, and transparency.
respuestas.c: It prevents the need for data residency compliance.
respuestas.d: It automatically guarantees 100% model accuracy.

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service provides a detailed history of resource configuration changes and relationships to support compliance auditing?

respuestas.a: AWS Config
respuestas.b: AWS Trusted Advisor
respuestas.c: Amazon Inspector
respuestas.d: AWS Artifact

preguntas.pregunta: In the Generative AI Security Scoping Matrix, which scope involves using publicly available generative AI tools without backend access or customization?

respuestas.a: Scope 3: Pretrained models
respuestas.b: Scope 4: Fine-tuned models
respuestas.c: Scope 5: Self-trained models
respuestas.d: Scope 1: Consumer applications

preguntas.pregunta: Which AWS service uses machine learning (ML) to detect and classify sensitive data like personally identifiable information (PII) or protected health information (PHI) across your environment?

respuestas.a: AWS Verified Permissions
respuestas.b: AWS Shield Advanced
respuestas.c: Amazon Macie
respuestas.d: Amazon SageMaker Role Manager

preguntas.pregunta: What is the purpose of a model card in the context of generative AI?

respuestas.a: To automatically retrain models with new data
respuestas.b: To manage access controls across cloud infrastructure
respuestas.c: To document the data sources, intended uses, risks, and limitations of a model
respuestas.d: To optimize compute resources for faster model deployment.

preguntas.pregunta: In a generative AI system, who always controls user data, regardless of application scope?

respuestas.a: The application provider
respuestas.b: The cloud service provider
respuestas.c: The data annotation team
respuestas.d: The customer or end user
